ranked_movies.title: Pulp Fiction
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited for its iconic 'Royale with Cheese' burger scene, which is used as the prime example of a legendary, dialogue-heavy food moment in cinema.

ranked_movies.title: District 9
ranked_movies.reason: Top-voted recommendation for a movie that teases a non-existent sequel, creating lasting audience anticipation.

ranked_movies.title: Mulholland Drive
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently mentioned as a mind-blowing film with an unpredictable plot and specifically highlighted for its frightening, non-violent Winkie's Diner scene.

ranked_movies.title: No Country for Old Men
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its frightening non-violent coin toss scene and as a prime example of characters making stupid, consequential decisions.

ranked_movies.title: Goodfellas
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted for its iconic prison dinner scene, a landmark food moment in film history.

ranked_movies.title: The Usual Suspects
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently recommended as a film with a mind-blowing, unpredictable plot twist.

ranked_movies.title: Shutter Island
ranked_movies.reason: Top-voted suggestion for a movie with an unpredictable plot twist that leaves viewers rethinking everything.

ranked_movies.title: Memento
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned multiple times as an incredible, mind-blowing film with an unpredictable narrative structure.

ranked_movies.title: Fargo
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a key example of a film where characters make extremely stupid decisions with dire consequences.

ranked_movies.title: Inglourious Basterds
ranked_movies.reason: Cited for its iconic apple strudel scene, a standout food moment in modern cinema.

ranked_movies.title: Dune
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed in the context of a 'chosen one' narrative with complex moral implications, mentioned multiple times.

ranked_movies.title: Dredd
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a film that far exceeded expectations and is considered one of the best action films of its decade.

ranked_movies.title: Certified Copy
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of deep analysis and a starting point for discussing filmmakers like Abbas Kiarostami; described as a favorite film.

ranked_movies.title: A Woman Under the Influence
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a lengthy, detailed analysis post exploring its use of Swan Lake as a thematic device and its portrayal of mental health.

ranked_movies.title: When Harry Met Sally
ranked_movies.reason: Instantly recommended for its iconic deli scene ('I'll have what she's having'), a legendary food moment.

ranked_movies.title: Pirates of the Caribbean
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed as a film based on a ride that wildly exceeded expectations and became a cultural juggernaut.

ranked_movies.title: Galaxy Quest
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a film that was funnier and more perfect than expected, with a beloved cast.

ranked_movies.title: Manchester by the Sea
ranked_movies.reason: The central point of a comparative analysis with 'Sorry, Baby', discussed for its portrayal of blue-collar trauma.

ranked_movies.title: Zodiac
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its frightening basement scene, a masterclass in building tension without violence.

ranked_movies.title: Garden State
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a discussion thread analyzing its use of the 'manic pixie dream girl' trope and its place in romantic cinema.
